sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 31

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 31:1
items.html:
(to the question) Derech Yerida [way of descent]
When the Lights spread from above downward in the Partzuf — for the Masach [screen], which is from below upward, spreads from itself and within itself into ten Sefirot and clothes in the discernment of a Guf [body], as is known — the Lights are then discerned as being in the way of descent. This is because they depend on the measure of the greatness of the Aviut [thickness]; and according to the measure of the greatness of the Aviut — that is, according to the depth of the sinking — so is the height of the level of the Light. Thus the Lights descend to the very bottom of the sinking, and according to the depth of their descent, so is the height of their level (see Answer 9). Therefore the name of this way of the Lights is the way of descent. (p. 777, Item 67).
